commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR preserves protected Codex route refs during non-interactive doctor/update flows, adds protected-route drift rollback, annotates cross-provider fallback transitions, updates tests, and documents the new behavior.

PR surface: Source +293, Tests +273, Docs 0. Total +566 across 14 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: current main runs non-interactive doctor repair through maybeRepairCodexRoutes, which rewrites openai-codex/* refs, and the linked issue plus PR body describe the resulting Codex harness failure. I did not run a live gateway reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] There are two narrow, mechanical repair targets in the PR branch before maintainer policy review can proceed.

target.title: fix(update): preserve protected Codex routes
target.body:
## Summary

- Preserve legacy `openai-codex/*` and `codex/*` protected model/runtime routes during non-interactive doctor repair instead of silently rewriting them to `openai/*`.
- Add update-time protected-route audit/rollback for core update and post-core finalization: if a protected Codex route is rewritten or deleted, restore the pre-update config and fail closed with `protected-route-drift`.
- Mark cross-provider/auth-route-changing fallbacks in fallback telemetry and keep route-changing fallback notices visible even when upstream verbose fallback output is off.
- Document the interactive-vs-non-interactive doctor behavior so operators know when routes are migrated versus preserved.
- Keep interactive `openclaw doctor --fix` migration behavior unchanged.

## Evidence

This is based on the live incident captured in #80628 and comment https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/80628#issuecomment-4585716866.

Observed on OpenClaw 2026.5.28:

- `openclaw doctor` touched `/root/.openclaw/openclaw.json` at `2026-05-31T04:29:14Z`.
- A previously working Codex setup had model refs rewritten from a Codex route to OpenAI-provider refs across `agents.defaults.model.primary` and specialist-agent `model.primary` / `fallbacks` entries.
- The next isolated `News Digest` cron run failed before reply under the Codex harness with:

```text
Requested agent harness "codex" does not support openai/gpt-5.5 (provider is not one of: codex).
```

Local remediation was to patch the config back to Codex model refs, remove `openai/gpt-5.5` from the allowed agent model sets, and let the gateway hot-reload. After remediation, `openclaw models status --json` reported `defaultModel` and `resolvedDefault` as `codex/gpt-5.5` with allowed `codex/gpt-5.5` and `xai/grok-4.3`.

This PR now covers the full failure mode from #80628: doctor preservation, update audit/rollback, missing protected-route deletion detection, and visibility for cross-provider fallback transitions.

Refs #80628, #88450, #88470.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Non-interactive doctor repair and update/finalize flows no longer silently rewrite or delete protected Codex route model/runtime refs.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw source checkout on this PR branch, isolated temporary `OPENCLAW_HOME` and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`, no production config or credentials.
- **Exact steps or command run after the patch:** Ran `pnpm openclaw doctor --non-interactive --fix` against a temp config containing `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`, then ran `pnpm openclaw update --json --no-restart` with `OPENCLAW_UPDATE_POST_CORE=1`, `OPENCLAW_UPDATE_POST_CORE_SOURCE_CONFIG_PATH`, and `OPENCLAW_UPDATE_POST_CORE_RESULT_PATH` against a temp pre/post config pair where protected Codex primary/fallback refs were rewritten and the model-scoped Codex runtime route was deleted.
- **Evidence after fix:** The terminal excerpts below show doctor preserving `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`, update exiting with `reason: "protected-route-drift"`, post-core result warnings including `agents.defaults.models.codex/gpt-5.5.agentRuntime.id: codex -> <missing>`, and the isolated config restored back to Codex routes.
- **Observed result after fix:** Doctor completed without migrating the protected route in non-interactive mode; update/finalize failed closed, wrote the drift result, restored the pre-update config, and did not continue plugin finalization.
- **What was not tested:** A live production gateway restart on the patched branch was not run; proof uses isolated temp config paths only.

- Behavior or issue addressed: Non-interactive repair and update/finalize flows no longer silently rewrite or delete protected Codex route model/runtime refs.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on this PR branch, Node v22.22.2, pnpm 11.2.2, isolated temporary `OPENCLAW_HOME` and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH` with no production config or credentials.

### Doctor preservation proof

Exact command: created a temporary config containing `agents.defaults.model: "openai-codex/gpt-5.5"`, then ran:

```sh
HOME="$tmpdir" OPENCLAW_HOME="$tmpdir" OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH="$config" timeout 90s pnpm openclaw doctor --non-interactive --fix
```

Terminal capture from the patched branch:

```text
doctor exit=0
config after doctor:
{
  "model": "openai-codex/gpt-5.5"
}

preservation warning lines:
43:│  - Legacy `openai-codex/*` model refs should be rewritten to            │
49:│  - Preserved legacy `openai-codex/*` model refs during non-interactive  │

last output lines:
Updated config: $OPENCLAW_HOME/.openclaw/openclaw.json
  Backup: $OPENCLAW_HOME/.openclaw/openclaw.json.bak
Backup: $OPENCLAW_HOME/.openclaw/openclaw.json.bak
│
└  Doctor complete.
```

Observed result: Doctor completed and wrote the isolated config while leaving the protected model route as `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`; the new preservation warning was emitted in doctor output.

### Update/finalize rollback proof

Exact command: created an isolated pre-update config with protected Codex primary, fallback, and model-scoped runtime routes, then created a post-update config where the primary/fallback were rewritten to `openai/*` and the Codex runtime route was deleted. Then ran the real post-core update/finalize path:

```sh
HOME="$tmpdir" \
OPENCLAW_HOME="$tmpdir" \
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH="$tmpdir/.openclaw/openclaw.json" \
OPENCLAW_UPDATE_POST_CORE=1 \
OPENCLAW_UPDATE_POST_CORE_CHANNEL=stable \
OPENCLAW_UPDATE_POST_CORE_SOURCE_CONFIG_PATH="$tmpdir/before.json" \
OPENCLAW_UPDATE_POST_CORE_RESULT_PATH="$tmpdir/post-core-result.json" \
timeout 90s pnpm openclaw update --json --no-restart
```

Terminal/log capture from the patched branch:

```text
exit=1
stdout:
{
  "status": "error",
  "mode": "unknown",
  "root": "/path/to/openclaw",
  "reason": "protected-route-drift",
  "steps": [],
  "durationMs": 0
}

post-core-result.json:
{
  "status": "error",
  "reason": "protected-route-drift",
  "changed": false,
  "warnings": [
    {
      "reason": "protected-route-drift",
      "message": "Protected model/runtime routes changed during update finalization; restored the pre-update config and stopped the update. agents.defaults.model.fallbacks.0: codex/gpt-5.4 -> openai/gpt-5.4; agents.defaults.model.primary: codex/gpt-5.5 -> openai/gpt-5.5; agents.defaults.models.codex/gpt-5.5.agentRuntime.id: codex -> <missing>",
      "guidance": ["Review the config change source, then rerun openclaw update."]
    }
  ],
  "sync": {
    "changed": false,
    "errors": [
      "agents.defaults.model.fallbacks.0: codex/gpt-5.4 -> openai/gpt-5.4",
      "agents.defaults.model.primary: codex/gpt-5.5 -> openai/gpt-5.5",
      "agents.defaults.models.codex/gpt-5.5.agentRuntime.id: codex -> <missing>"
    ]
  }
}

config after finalize:
{
  "agents": {
    "defaults": {
      "model": {
        "primary": "codex/gpt-5.5",
        "fallbacks": ["codex/gpt-5.4"]
      },
      "models": {
        "codex/gpt-5.5": {
          "agentRuntime": { "id": "codex" }
        }
      }
    }
  },
  "meta": {
    "lastTouchedVersion": "2026.5.31",
    "lastTouchedAt": "<redacted timestamp>"
  }
}
```

Observed result: The update/finalize process exited non-zero with `protected-route-drift`, wrote the post-core result file with both rewritten routes and the deleted runtime route, restored the pre-update Codex config, and did not continue plugin update/finalization.

What was not tested: A live production gateway restart on the patched branch was not run; proof uses isolated temp config paths only.

## Tests

Latest validation on head `51390655b51e` after rebasing onto `origin/main` `5b310a7b27f`:

- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm check:test-types`
- `pnpm deadcode:dependencies && pnpm deadcode:unused-files && pnpm deadcode:report:ci:ts-unused`
- `env -u OPENCLAW_SERVICE_MARKER -u OPENCLAW_SERVICE_KIND node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/cli/update-cli.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.core-send.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.send-validation.test.ts` — 159 tests passed
- `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 env -u OPENCLAW_SERVICE_MARKER -u OPENCLAW_SERVICE_KIND node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec.path.test.ts src/agents/compaction-planning-worker.test.ts src/agents/code-mode.test.ts` — 130 tests passed
target.author: pfrederiksen

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
